note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. sec-secrets owns deep credential/API-key/token/private-key detection across source, config, env files, and the FULL git history (gitleaks/trufflehog), with rotation guidance. Negatives are near-misses that share the security/secret vocabulary but belong to a sibling (sec-gate, sec-scan, sec-ci, sec-deps, sec-iac, sec-fix, sec-compliance) or a plain commit/config/explain action.

queries.text: scan the repo for leaked secrets and API keys
we're about to open-source this repo — check the whole git history for any committed credentials first
did I accidentally commit an AWS access key somewhere? run trufflehog or gitleaks over everything
post-incident triage: an old .env with a Stripe live key got pushed — find every place a secret leaked and tell me what to rotate
check config/ and all the .env.* files for hardcoded passwords and tokens
our security team asked me to prove there are no private keys or DB creds anywhere in the codebase or history
secret detection sweep before the prod deploy please — source, configs, and past commits
someone pasted a slack webhook token into a test file weeks ago, dig through the git log and find any leaked keys like that
look for any exposed credentials — jwt secrets, oauth client secrets, gcp service-account json, that kind of thing
manager wants a dedup'd report of every hardcoded api key in the repo with remediation and rotation steps
quick security check on my staged diff before I commit
queries.note: fast diff-scoped pre-commit gate → sec-gate, not a full code+history secret sweep

queries.text: run a full OWASP Top 10 audit of the whole service before we ship to prod
queries.note: comprehensive whole-codebase audit → sec-scan (which orchestrates secrets among others)

queries.text: our GitHub Actions workflow echoes a token into the build log — audit the pipeline for secret exposure
queries.note: secret handling scoped to CI pipeline config → sec-ci

queries.text: audit package.json and the lockfile for vulnerable dependencies and typosquats
queries.note: dependency CVE/license audit → sec-deps, no secrets involved

queries.text: our Terraform hardcodes a DB password in the .tf — review the IaC for weak secrets management
queries.note: infra config / secrets-management review → sec-iac, not a repo/history scan

queries.text: gitleaks flagged a leaked key in auth.py — write me a patch that removes it and add a regression test
queries.note: remediating an already-found finding → sec-fix, the detection is done

queries.text: move the API key out of the source and read it from an env var instead
queries.note: a plain refactor/edit to externalize a known secret, not a detection scan

queries.text: explain how our app loads secrets from Vault at startup
queries.note: understanding secret-loading logic → explain, not a leak scan

queries.text: map our secrets-storage controls against OWASP ASVS for the compliance review
add .env and *.pem to .gitignore so we stop tracking credential files
queries.note: gitignore config to prevent future commits, not a scan for existing leaks — plain edit

queries.text: before I commit, but also go back through the whole git history — I think a secret got pushed months ago and I need to find every occurrence
queries.note: mentions committing but the ask is a deep history-wide secret hunt → must beat sec-gate

queries.text: I need a thorough secrets-only audit across source, configs, and past commits — not the whole OWASP thing, just leaked credentials
queries.note: explicitly scoped to secrets across code+history → must beat sec-scan

queries.text: gitleaks already found a hardcoded key in src/config.ts — now generate a tested fix and rotation plan
queries.note: detection is done; producing the tested patch for the finding is sec-fix's job

queries.text: give me a fast security sanity-check on the changes I just staged before pushing — catch any injected secret or credential
queries.note: diff-scoped, pre-push, fast gate over staged changes → sec-gate wins over the full history scan
